text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 59-year-old male with known metastatic sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma (KRAS G12D mutant, MSS), currently on second-line FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab (reduced-dose irinotecan 120 mg/m2 following prior Grade 3 toxicity). Restaging evaluation after 8 cycles of second-line therapy. Known hepatic and pulmonary metastatic sites. Comparison provided with prior CT from approximately 6 weeks prior and with the most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis at the time of progression initiation.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the intravenous administration of 100 mL of iohexol contrast agent. Images were acquired in the portal venous phase through the abdomen and pelvis and in the standard thoracic phase through the chest. Axial, coronal, and sagittal reconstructions reviewed on PACS. No prior allergic reactions to contrast on file.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The previously identified bilateral pulmonary nodules are again identified and demonstrate overall stability in size compared with the prior examination.
- Right upper lobe: a subpleural, non-calcified soft tissue nodule in the posterior segment of the right upper lobe measures 7 mm in greatest dimension (previously 7 mm; unchanged). Margins remain smooth and well-circumscribed. No surrounding ground-glass halo. No internal necrosis or cavitation.
- Right lower lobe: a small well-defined nodule in the superior segment of the right lower lobe measures 5 mm in greatest dimension (previously 5 mm; unchanged). No associated consolidation.
- Left upper lobe: a nodule in the left upper lobe apical-posterior segment measures 5 mm in greatest dimension (previously 5 mm, with a prior measurement of 6 mm at the time of the first FOLFIRI restaging — this nodule has demonstrated very slight interval diminution and now appears stable at 5 mm). No new nodularity identified.
- No new pulmonary nodules or masses are identified in either lung field.
- No pleural effusions bilaterally. No pneumothorax.
- The central airways are patent to the level of the segmental bronchi. No endobronchial lesions.
- No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy above the size threshold for pathologic enlargement. The previously noted absence of FDG-avid pulmonary hilar nodes (from initial PET-CT staging) remains consistent with the current CT appearance.
- No pericardial effusion. The cardiac silhouette is within normal limits for size. Mild aortic atherosclerotic calcification noted, unchanged.
- Imaged osseous structures of the thorax: no suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions. No rib fractures.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in contour with no capsular retraction. Hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous in background attenuation. The previously identified hepatic metastases are again identified at their expected locations. No new hypodense or rim-enhancing hepatic lesions are seen.
- Segment VI lesion: hypodense, mildly heterogeneous mass in the posterior right lobe (segment VI/VII junction area) measuring 2.0 x 1.7 cm in axial dimension (previously 2.0 x 1.7 cm; stable). The lesion demonstrates peripheral rim enhancement on portal venous phase imaging with a relatively hypovascular center, consistent with the known metastatic deposit. No new internal necrosis or septations identified since prior imaging.
- Segment II lesion: small hypodense nodule in the lateral segment of the left lobe (segment II) measuring 0.9 x 0.8 cm in axial dimension (previously 0.9 x 0.8 cm; stable). Enhancement characteristics unchanged — thin rim of peripheral enhancement on portal venous phase.
- Segment IV lesion: hypodense lesion at the medial segment of the left lobe (segment IV) measuring 1.1 x 0.9 cm in axial dimension (previously 1.1 x 0.9 cm; stable). Unchanged morphology.
- Segment VII lesion: the previously noted near-complete response of the segment VII lesion is maintained — no discrete measurable lesion is identified at this location. There is a tiny residual area of hypoattenuation measuring less than 5 mm which likely represents post-treatment fibrotic change or residual minimal viable tumor; this has not appreciably changed over the course of multiple prior examinations.
- No new satellite lesions or hepatic capsular irregularity. The hepatic veins and portal vein are patent with normal enhancement. No biliary dilatation. No hepatic vascular encasement by tumor.

Periportal and Perihepatic Lymph Nodes: Small lymph nodes are again noted in the portocaval space and in the perihepatic region. The largest previously noted perihepatic lymph node measures approximately 1.0 cm in short axis (previously noted to be up to 1.3 cm at the time of progression — this node has slightly decreased in size and has not increased). No new pathologically enlarged lymph nodes are identified in the gastrohepatic ligament or hepatoduodenal ligament.

Gallbladder and Biliary Tree: The gallbladder is surgically absent (cholecystectomy; previously noted). No biliary ductal dilatation. No pneumobilia.

Spleen: Normal in size at approximately 11.2 cm in craniocaudal dimension. No focal splenic lesions.

Pancreas: The pancreas demonstrates normal morphology with no ductal dilatation. No peripancreatic stranding. No focal pancreatic masses.

Adrenal Glands: Bilateral adrenal glands appear normal. No new adrenal nodules or enlargement. The previously reported absence of adrenal metastases at initial staging continues to hold.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys enhance symmetrically with no hydronephrosis. Simple cortical cysts are noted bilaterally (unchanged from prior). No new renal masses. No hydroureter. The right kidney shows a 1.4 cm simple cortical cyst in the lower pole (unchanged).

Aorta and Great Vessels: The abdominal aorta and its major branches are patent with mild infrarenal atherosclerotic calcification, unchanged. No aneurysmal dilatation.

PELVIS:

Postoperative Changes from Prior Sigmoid Colectomy: The pelvis demonstrates expected postoperative findings consistent with the recent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy performed approximately 6 weeks prior to this examination. There is a primary anastomosis visible in the left lower pelvis. Small amount of presacral and pericolic postoperative inflammatory stranding is noted, which is within expected limits for the time frame from surgery. No discrete fluid collection, abscess, or anastomotic leak identified. No obvious pelvic hematoma.

No discrete pelvic soft tissue masses or evidence of pelvic nodal disease are identified. The urinary bladder is decompressed but unremarkable in wall morphology. No hydronephrosis on the pelvic views.

No suspicious pelvic osseous lesions.

Bony Structures (Abdomen and Pelvis): No new lytic or blastic osseous lesions. No pathologic fractures. The sacrum, iliac wings, and visualized lumbar spine are without suspicious focal lesion.

Ascites: No free intraperitoneal fluid. No peritoneal nodularity or omental caking to suggest peritoneal carcinomatosis.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Metastatic sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma — overall disease burden appears unchanged from the prior CT examination approximately 6 weeks ago, reflecting a picture of maintained disease stability on current FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab therapy.

2. The three previously documented bilateral pulmonary nodules (right upper lobe 7 mm, right lower lobe 5 mm, left upper lobe 5 mm) are stable in size and morphology with no new pulmonary nodularity identified. No evidence of intrathoracic progression.

3. The three measurable hepatic metastases (segment VI at 2.0 x 1.7 cm, segment II at 0.9 x 0.8 cm, and segment IV at 1.1 x 0.9 cm) are unchanged compared with the prior examination. The segment VII lesion, which achieved near-complete response during earlier induction chemotherapy, remains undetectable as a discrete mass.

4. The previously identified perihepatic/portocaval lymph node has shown very modest interval decrease in size (now approximately 1.0 cm, down from 1.3 cm), with no new regional adenopathy.

5. Expected postoperative changes following recent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy. No evidence of anastomotic complication, pelvic collection, or local residual/recurrent pelvic disease.

6. No evidence of new hepatic, pulmonary, osseous, peritoneal, or adrenal metastases.

Overall, the imaging findings are most consistent with a plateau of response — the disease appears to have stabilized without clear evidence of further shrinkage or frank progression at the time of this examination.
